question: How many annual leave days am I entitled to?
answer: Full-time employees at Sujith are entitled to 18 days of paid annual leave per calendar year. Part-time employees receive leave on a pro-rata basis.

question: How do I apply for maternity leave?
answer: To apply for maternity leave, submit a formal request to your HR Business Partner at least 8 weeks before the expected start date. Sujith provides 26 weeks of paid maternity leave as per policy.

question: What is the process to update my bank account details for payroll?
answer: Log in to the HR Portal (hrportal.sujith.com), navigate to 'My Profile' > 'Payroll Details', and update your bank account information. Changes take effect in the next payroll cycle.

question: When is the payroll disbursement date each month?
answer: Salaries at Sujith are disbursed on the 28th of each month. If the 28th falls on a weekend or public holiday, payment is made on the preceding working day.

question: How can I request a salary certificate or employment letter?
answer: You can request official documents like salary certificates or employment letters through the HR Portal under 'Documents & Letters'. Processing time is 2–3 working days.

question: What health insurance benefits do I get as an employee?
answer: Sujith provides comprehensive health insurance coverage for all full-time employees, including hospitalization, outpatient care, dental, and vision. Dependents (spouse and up to 2 children) can be added at subsidized rates.

question: How do I report a workplace grievance or harassment complaint?
answer: All grievances can be reported confidentially through the HR Ethics Hotline (1800-555-HR01) or by emailing ethics@sujith.com. All complaints are investigated within 10 working days.

question: What is Sujith's policy on remote work?
answer: Sujith follows a hybrid work model — employees are required to be in office at least 3 days per week. Work-from-home eligibility is determined by your role and approved by your manager.

question: How do I claim reimbursements for travel or business expenses?
answer: Submit expense claims via the Expense Portal (expense.sujith.com) within 30 days of incurring the cost. Attach original receipts. Approved claims are reimbursed within 7 working days.

question: What is the notice period if I decide to resign?
answer: The standard notice period at Sujith is 30 days for general employees and 60 days for managerial and above roles. Notice should be submitted in writing to your manager and HR.

question: How do I enroll for the Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP)?
answer: ESPP enrollment opens every January and July. Visit the Benefits Portal (benefits.sujith.com) and enroll during the open window. You can contribute 1–10% of your base salary.

question: Is there a referral bonus program?
answer: Yes! Sujith offers a referral bonus of ₹25,000 for every successful hire through the employee referral program. The bonus is paid after the referred employee completes 90 days.

question: How do I check my remaining leave balance?
answer: Your leave balance is visible in the HR Portal under 'Leave Management' > 'My Leave Balance'. It is updated in real-time whenever a leave request is approved or rejected.
